title: Implement scoped kill-switch engagement, disarm and denial proofs
goal: Keep the canonical emergency stop fail-closed while replacing the binary global blockade with an evidence-bound two-axis model of scope and posture, protecting the marker from generic mutation, adding typed engagement and disarm, and isolating all denial proofs from production state.

acceptance.assertion: The exact marker SHA-256 1a66d92ea0d5ec2ef463545bf58a181eaca5f7955c3d502dc48810bca8f674f3 is moved reversibly out of the canonical path by an external hash-bound helper; marker absence, quarantine identity, audit validity, recovery readiness and operator health are read back.

Blockades use independent scope and posture axes. Supported scopes are path, capability, task, owner, repository, service, host and global; supported postures are observe, preflight_required, mutation_freeze and hard_stop. Matching records compose monotonically to the strongest applicable posture.

Global hard_stop is reserved for global trust failures such as invalid audit or runtime provenance, compromised broker or recovery identity, an external environment kill switch, or damage with credible host-wide reach. Local CI, task, lease, path or service failures cannot create a global hard stop.
KILL_SWITCH_PATH is rejected by generic create, replace, remove, destroy, terminal and indirect execution surfaces regardless of trusted_owner; tests prove no other protected-path contract regresses.

A dedicated engage operation creates only a schema-valid regular single-link 0600 record with bounded reason, trigger class, scope, posture, evidence identity and complete tool, request, session, task and owner provenance, using create-only semantics and an audit receipt.

A dedicated disarm operation is the only in-band exception: it requires exact blockade identity and record SHA-256, exact scope, environment switch off, valid audit chain, valid deployed provenance, fresh canonical recovery readiness, safe marker identity, reversible quarantine, fsync and absence readback.

All ordinary mutations denied by an applicable mutation_freeze or hard_stop remain denied; wrong hash, scope mismatch, symlink, hardlink, broad mode, malformed record, environment kill switch, invalid audit, stale recovery or deployment drift deny disarm before mutation. Immutable read, status, audit and tightly bound recovery remain available.

The deployed artifact is exercised in a transient isolated HOME and STATE_DIR for blockade and denial proofs. The probe refuses the canonical production path and leaves it byte-for-byte unchanged on success, exception and signal cleanup paths.
Focused tests and full validation pass; the complete current GitHub diff is exported as a downloadable SHA-256-bound artifact; diff-bound critical self-review passes before merge.

After merge, the immutable runtime is deployed and provenance-valid; live isolated checks prove scoped decisions, typed engagement, normal mutation denial and typed disarm without leaving a production test marker.

metadata.source: Operator incident, live audit and issue heimgewebe/grabowski#215 on 2026-07-14
root_cause: generic create therefore reached the canonical kill-switch path
the blanket mutation guard blocks the marker's own cleanup path
the T030 denial proof used production state and lacked atomic cleanup

previous_block.reason: The production test marker blocked Grabowski mutations while the central MCP source was independently leased.

implementation_sequence: build and test the conflict-free blockade core in disjoint new files
wait for authoritative release of the exact central MCP and capability leases
integrate typed runtime surfaces without overwriting foreign work
export diff, review, merge, deploy and verify in isolated state
coordination_constraints: src/grabowski_mcp.py and related generated surfaces remain leased by chatgpt-workspace-lifecycle-cleanup-v1-20260714 at task activation
no lease override, foreign worktree mutation or concurrent edit is permitted
the external environment kill switch remains outside in-band disarm authority
does_not_establish: permission to clear a different marker
permission to bypass environment kill switch
permission to modify foreign worktrees or leases
